title: OpenPath cross-platform firewall + DNS sinkhole contract scenario
description: Language-neutral scenario fixtures encoding the enforcement invariants of linux/lib/{firewall-rule-helpers,dns-dnsmasq,dns-runtime}.sh and windows/lib/internal/Firewall.Policy.ps1. Executed by tests/e2e/contract-scenarios/run-contract-scenarios.sh (Linux, real dnsmasq+iptables inside the E2E Docker lane) and tests/e2e/ci/run-windows-contract-scenarios.ps1 (Windows, real firewall on the self-hosted lab runner) plus the mocked unit rungs (tests/contract-scenarios.bats, windows/tests/Windows.ContractScenarios.Tests.ps1). Flag keys are the canonical linux/lib/defaults.conf variable names; Linux maps each key K to the OPENPATH_K environment override, Windows maps DOH_BLOCK_ENABLED to config.enableDohIpBlocking and treats SINKHOLE_FAST_FAIL / IPV6_FIREWALL_ENABLED / ALLOW_SET_EGRESS_ENABLED as linux-only (scenarios overriding those must be linux-scoped). fail-open/fail-closed semantics follow docs/adr/0011-unified-failure-semantics.md and are settled: scenarios encode them, they do not re-litigate them.

invariantSource.description: File:line or commit reference of the prose invariant this scenario encodes

whitelist.description: Domains treated as the downloaded whitelist for this scenario. Empty array = the empty-whitelist / fetch-failure posture (ADR-0011).

setup.description: Procedural pre-step implemented by the platform runner. persisted-upstream: persist the detected upstream to ORIGINAL_DNS_FILE, apply the firewall (owner-confining upstream :53 to dnsmasq's uid), then re-run detect_primary_dns and require the persisted value back (regression 8fe4cbc0).
